project.title: Ingreso a la Uni / SAINS
project.one_liner: Legacy PHP web platform for university entrance-exam preparation, enrollment, and student self-study workflows.
project.description: Legacy PHP application that combines a public marketing site, course and guide registration microsites, an authenticated SAINS learning portal, and admin/upload modules for university entrance-exam preparation services.

setup_steps: This repository does not include a one-command local setup; review composer.json, ingresoa_ingreso.sql, php/database.php, and sains/Connections/sains.php before attempting a local run.
Install the PHP dependencies declared in composer.json.
Provision a MySQL database from ingresoa_ingreso.sql.
Review file-based configuration under php/ and sains/Connections/ before serving the application.

cv_bullets.bullet: Built and maintained a PHP/MySQL web platform that combined public marketing pages, student registration, and authenticated learning flows for university entrance-exam preparation.
cv_bullets.notes: Public site and authenticated portal are explicitly separated in the repository.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented multiple authentication and onboarding paths, including email/password, Google OAuth, and Facebook-based signup/login.
cv_bullets.notes: Social and email-based flows are explicit in application source.

cv_bullets.bullet: Added transactional email workflows for contact requests, new-user registration, and guide-download confirmations using PHPMailer and native mail handlers.
cv_bullets.notes: Email sending is directly implemented in the server-side handlers.

cv_bullets.bullet: Delivered an online study experience with video playback, exam simulators, results/history views, and progress tracking against watched-time thresholds.
cv_bullets.notes: Watched-time checks and cron-based follow-up logic are explicit in the code.

cv_bullets.bullet: Integrated payment-related enrollment flows, including Conekta-based OXXO cash order generation and course-payment instructions for online registration.
cv_bullets.notes: Conekta usage is explicit; broader payment instructions are documented in course pages.

cv_bullets.bullet: Supported student photograph and payment-receipt uploads through dedicated server-side upload handlers and file-storage endpoints.
cv_bullets.notes: Both modules instantiate dedicated UploadHandler classes.

cv_bullets.bullet: Maintained a separate admin surface for operational workflows such as users, payments, exams, questions, coupons, and administrator management.
cv_bullets.notes: Capabilities are explicit from the admin entry points and module filenames.

cv_bullets.bullet: Packaged upload-related modules in Dockerized PHP 7/Apache environments with GD and Imagick enabled for image handling.
cv_bullets.notes: Docker coverage is limited to upload modules rather than the full application.

cv_bullets.bullet: Created UAEM-focused microsites for course registration and guide-download funnels that feed into the broader SAINS ecosystem.
cv_bullets.notes: These directories operate as standalone campaign flows inside the same repository.

cv_bullets.bullet: Implemented a scheduled follow-up routine that records cron execution and checks whether users met expected daily study-time targets.
cv_bullets.notes: The cron file writes to diario_cronjob and compares watched-time values against threshold rules.

red_flags.issue: Committed third-party credentials and API keys appear in application source files.
red_flags.notes: Keep them out of public documentation and rotate or externalize them before reuse.

red_flags.issue: Core application code mixes legacy mysql_* calls and PDO-based database access.
red_flags.notes: This increases maintenance risk and makes data access behavior less consistent.

red_flags.issue: Local setup is manual and spread across manifests, SQL dumps, and module-specific Docker files.
red_flags.notes: The repository does not provide a documented root-level bootstrap flow.
